expectations.text: The agent implements the reviewer's request by updating SKILL.md to use --body-file instead of --body
expectations.evidence: The agent simulated editing skills/pr-comments/SKILL.md Step 5, replacing 'gh pr edit {pr_number} --body "$UPDATED_BODY"' with a --body-file pattern using mktemp, printf, and 'gh pr edit {pr_number} --body-file "$body_file"'.

expectations.text: The agent detects that specs/23-pr-comments-reduce-review-rounds/plan.md still references the old --body pattern after fixing SKILL.md
expectations.evidence: After fixing SKILL.md, the agent explicitly scanned 'PR diff files for: --body "$UPDATED_BODY" pattern' and found the same old pattern in specs/23-pr-comments-reduce-review-rounds/plan.md, noting 'This file is in the PR diff but was not updated when SKILL.md was changed.'

expectations.text: The plan.md drift fix is included in the same commit as the SKILL.md change, not committed separately
expectations.evidence: The agent staged both files ('git add skills/pr-comments/SKILL.md' and 'git add specs/23-pr-comments-reduce-review-rounds/plan.md') and committed them together in a single commit, with the simulated output showing '2 files changed, 8 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)'.

expectations.text: The commit credits copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot] via a Co-authored-by trailer
expectations.evidence: Although the simulation included a Co-authored-by trailer in the commit message, a general assistant without the pr-comments skill would not reliably add a Co-authored-by trailer crediting a bot reviewer. This attribution convention is driven by the pr-comments skill's explicit protocol for crediting reviewers. In a typical without_skill run, the agent would craft a standard commit message without this bot-attribution trailer. The inclusion in this simulation was incidental and not driven by an explicit workflow rule.
